Article.description: The paragraphs of the story after the lead.
The products on which we do assessments or products closely associated with our assessed products
Article.example: Agriculture, Petroleum, Propylene, Coalbed methane, CFC

Article.description: The legal holder of the rights to distribution and copy for this content
The key regions, countries and their political subregions referenced or reported on in Platts authored content.
Article.example: Korea, North; Canada; Europe; Michigan; Germany; Russia

Article.description: Short character limited summary of content that is often attached to an accompanying piece of content for reader-centric (attention grabbing) content ID purposes.  First thing a reader usually sees or is presented with.
Unique ID for a piece of content
language that the content has been written in or into which it has been translated
The page number of which this content is associated with in its delivery over Product serviceLines
The timestamp of when the content is first published.
A organizational view of the commodities market which groups commodities based on user permissions.
The named  product that contains single pieces of content delivered.
Latest timestamp on a piece of content that has gone through changes after initial publication.
The incremental count of versions returned into the repository after initial publication. Denoted by X.Y where X increments on major updates, and Y on minor updates.

A heard attribute describing important information that cannot be captured in the heard, such as if the heard was before or after the MOC process. Mostly contained in brackets at the end of the heard but not exclusive.
A heard attribute describing the brand of the commodity being traded.
A heard attribute describing additional information of the brand of the commodity being traded.
A heard attribute describing the entity buying the item/activity.
A heard attribute denoting the certification (i.e. standard) the heard is descibing (related to carbon market).
A heard attribute describing the co-benefits of climate change mitigation (in relation to the heard).
A heard attribute describing the brand of the commodity being traded.
A heard attribute describing additional information of the brand of the commodity being traded.
A heard attribute describing the money system used for a reported price.
A heard attribute describing the grade of the commodity being traded.

A heard attribute describing the universal set of rules and guidelines that characterizes a transaction between two parties, usually the party exporting goods and the party importing them. They tell each party concisely what is expected of them in selling and in contract negotiations.
A heard attribute describing the geographical location (i.e. region) describing where the commodity has been delivered (or loaded).
A heard attribute describing a heard's differential or flat price (maximum price range).
A heard attribute describing the maximum volume of oil identified in a heard (range).
A heard attribute describing a heard's differential or flat price (minimum price range).
A heard attribute describing the minimum volume of oil identified in a heard (range).
A heard attribute describing a heard's differential or flat price (exact).
A heard attribute describing whether a heard is priced on a floating or fixed basis.
A heard attribute describing whether a heard has an exact price or a price range.

A heard attribute describing the value of a variety of commodities in a specific location as a flat price and as a differential against common pricing bases. A pricing basis may also be another grade, commonly if the price is 'spread' between two grades.

A heard attribute describing if the heard is using a 'Pricing Basis' outside the current 'Pricing Basis' month. If so, the price reporter is required to include it in the heard. The 'Pricing Basis Timing' will always come just before the 'Pricing Basis' attribute and will be either a month, or labeled as front-month, second-month.
A heard attribute describing the producer of the commodity being reported.
A heard attribute describing the Public quality of the commodity being reported.
A heard attribute describing the quality specification of the commodity being traded (in relation to the heard).
A heard attribute describing the type of quality specification of the commodity being traded (in relation to the heard).
A heard attribute describing the tolerance percentage of aluminum.
A heard attribute describing the price unit of measurement.
A heard attribute capturing the volume of oil identified in a heard (exact). Values can be captured in using for numeric range (1,000,000) or combined with the Volume Quantity field (1.08).
A heard attribute giving additional information concerning volume.
Article.example: equals, around, above, below or between

Article.description: A heard attribute describing the volume unit of measure (always comes directly after volume).
A heard attribute representing a measure of certainty regarding the validity of the heard.
A heard attribute describing the type of contract for a reported trade (in relation to the heard).
A heard attribute denoting whether the emissions units are CORSIA eligible.
A heard attribute describing the country where the commodity has been delivered (or loaded).
A heard attribute describing the subtype of credit for a reported trade (in relation to the heard).
A heard attribute describing the type of credit for a reported trade (in relation to the heard).
A heard attribute describing the money system used for a reported price.
A heard attribute describing the date on which the activity has happened/ the commodity has been traded.
A heard attribute describing the delivery date/time window of the commodity traded.
A heard attribute describing the geographical location (i.e. region) describing where the commodity has been delivered.
A heard attribute denoting the delivery qualifier relevant to the commodity being traded.
A heard attribute describing the dimension type of the commodity traded.
A heard attribute describing the dimension UOM of the commodity traded.
A heard attribute describing the dimensions of the commodity traded.
A heard attribute describing the discharge rate of the commodity traded.
A heard attribute describing a note from an editor in relation to the heard.
A heard attribute denoting the institution/ marketplace where securities, commodities, derivatives and other financial instruments are traded.
Article.example: Platts US transaction premium financial futures on CME Globex

Article.description: A heard attribute describing the grade of the commodity being traded.
A heard attribute describing the quality of the grade whether its normal or premium.
A heard attribute giving additional information to help customers understand the type of transaction in the heard.
A heard attribute describing the day or date the heard was received outside of 'Today'.
A heard attribute describing the type of transactional activity being heard across the market.
A heard attribute capturing if a heard has been updated since being verified and transmitted to the market, and one (or more) attribute(s) has been updated.
A heard attribute describing the process used in relation to the commodity in the heard.

A heard attribute describing the universal set of rules and guidelines that characterizes a transaction between two parties, usually the party exporting goods and the party importing them. They tell each party concisely what is expected of them in selling and in contract negotiations.
A heard attribute describing the independent system operator entity of the project the PPA contract relates to.
Article.example: MISO (Midcontinent ISO), ERCOT (Electric Reliability Council of Texas)

Article.description: A heard attribute describing the contract dates of the item/activity. Can be loaded or delivered.
A heard attribute denoting the laycan qualifier relevant to the commodity being traded.
A heard attribute describing the load rate of the commodity traded.
A heard attribute describing the geographical location (i.e. region) describing where the commodity has been delivered (or loaded).
A heard attribute describing the number of exchange lots per month to be delivered over the course of the delivery period.
A heard attribute describing the market (i.e. product) related to the heard.
A heard attribute describing the type of market in relation to the heard.
A heard attribute describing a heard's differential or flat price (maximum price range).
A heard attribute describing the maximum volume of oil identified in a heard (range).
A heard attribute describing the methodology for price assessments.
A heard attribute describing a heard's differential or flat price (minimum price range).
A heard attribute describing the minimum volume of oil identified in a heard (range).
A heard attribute describing the geographical location (i.e. region) where the commodity originated.
A heard attribute describing the geographical location (i.e. region) describing where the commodity has been loaded.
A heard attribute describing how and when a customer pays for goods or services.
A heard attribute describing the Platform of the commodity being reported.
A heard attribute describing a heard's differential or flat price (exact).
A heard attribute describing whether a heard is priced on a floating or fixed basis.
A heard attribute describing whether a heard has an exact price or a price range.
A heard attribute giving additional information concerning a price.

A heard attribute describing the value of a variety of commodities in a specific location as a flat price and as a differential against common pricing bases. A pricing basis may also be another grade, commonly if the price is 'spread' between two grades.
A heard attribute describing the relevant futures contract month letter code, which is published next to the basis value.

A heard attribute describing if the heard is using a 'Pricing Basis' outside the current 'Pricing Basis' month. If so, the price reporter is required to include it in the heard. The 'Pricing Basis Timing' will always come just before the 'Pricing Basis' attribute and will be either a month, or labeled as front-month, second-month.
A heard attribute giving additional information concerning a price.
A heard attribute describing the producer of the commodity being reported.
A heard attribute giving the ID of the project the carbon credits are derived from defined by the certifier.
A heard attribute describing the Public quality of the commodity being reported.
A heard attribute describing the quality (i.e. grade) of the commodity being reported.
A heard attribute denoting the quantity qualifier relevant to the commodity being traded.
A heard attribute denoting the entity selling the item/activity.
A heard attribute describing the market price index used for settlement pricing.
A heard attribute describing the recognized electrical location within the relevant ISO market of the project the PPA contract relates to.
Article.example: MISO Indiana Hub, SPP North Hub

Article.description: A heard attribute denoting the shape of the commodity being traded (mainly related to metals).
A heard attribute denoting the date on which the commodity has been/will be shipped.
A heard attribute describing the shipment type of the commodity traded.
A heard attribute denoting the entity the heard is coming from.
A heard attribute describing the source qualifier for the source of the commodity being traded (in relation to the heard).
A heard attribute denoting the geographical location of the the heard is coming from.
A heard attribute denoting the type of source (i.e. broker, buyer, trader, etc.) the heard is coming from.
A heard attribute describing the quality specification of the commodity being traded (in relation to the heard).
A heard attribute describing the type of quality specification of the commodity being traded (in relation to the heard).
A heard attribute describing the brand of the commodity being traded.
A heard attribute describing additional information of the brand of the commodity being traded.
A heard attribute denoting the certification (i.e. standard) of the carbon credit the heard is pricing as a differential or spread to.
A heard attribute denoting whether or not the carbon credits in the heard can be used to offset emissions under the Carbon Offsetting and Reduction Scheme for International Aviation.
A heard attribute describing the subtype of the carbon credit the heard is pricing as a differential or spread to.
A heard attribute describing the type of the carbon credit the heard is pricing as a differential or spread to.
A heard attribute describing the producer of the commodity being reported.
A heard attribute describing the Public quality of the commodity being reported.
A heard attribute denoting the qualifier for the spread.
A heard attribute describing the region of the carbon credit the heard is pricing as a differential or spread to.
A heard attribute describing the quality specification of the commodity being traded (in relation to the heard).
A heard attribute describing the type of quality specification of the commodity being traded (in relation to the heard).
A heard attribute describing the 'spread' vintage of the commodity traded (carbon credits).
A heard attribute describing the current lifecycle of a transaction (bid, offer, negotiation or trade).
A heard attribute describing the technology used to generate electricity in a PPA contract.
A heard attribute describing the duration of the executed PPA contract.
A heard attribute describing the start date of energy delivery for the negotiated contract.
A heard attribute denoting whether a price is a differential between two months rather than a pricing basis.
A heard attribute describing the timing of the year (month or spot) the trade is taking place in.
A heard attribute describing the type the timing is referring to (e.g. delivery, loading, release).
A heard attribute describing the tolerance percentage of aluminum.
A heard attribute describing the total number of exchange lots to be delivered over the course of the delivery period.
A heard attribute denoting the terms, quality and conditions of the trade.
A heard attribute denoting the date on which the commodity has been/will be traded.
A heard attribute describing the different types of vehicles and vessels (and the volume they can carry) used to transport a commodity.
A heard attribute describing the price unit of measurement.
A heard attribute describing the vintage of the commodity traded (carbon credits).
A heard attribute capturing the volume of oil identified in a heard (exact). Values can be captured in using for numeric range (1,000,000) or combined with the Volume Quantity field (1.08).
A heard attribute giving additional information concerning volume.
Article.example: equals, around, above, below or between

Article.description: A heard attribute describing the quantity unit for volume.
A heard attribute describing the volume unit of measure (always comes directly after volume).
A heard attribute describing the type of weight of the commodity being traded (in relation to the heard).
